directorPlan.scene_setting: A small music theory worktable beside the practice-room sign-out desk, ten minutes before the next group class.
directorPlan.scene_opening: Two empty label boxes sit under a large printed 3/4, with three blank beat tiles and one quarter-note card laid beside the bar line stencil.
directorPlan.relationship: A notation clinic tutor and a focused learner finishing a worksheet check before class starts.
directorPlan.stakes: The learner needs to label the 3 and the 4 correctly and place one bar line without treating the time signature as a speed marking.
directorPlan.locale: Plain institutional English in a practice-room clinic, with concise worktable language rather than casual coaching patter.
directorPlan.register: Compact, object-led, mildly pressured, dry rather than warm; speech follows the cards, boxes, and marks.
directorPlan.stage_direction_style_prompt: Use one concrete object action or material arrangement that changes what the speakers can honestly say.
directorPlan.voice_constraints: No generic encouragement formulae, no musical pep-talk voice, no 'great question' or 'your instinct is right' pattern. Speakers should name visible marks, boxes, tiles, bars, beats, and notes. Keep the first exchange routine: top number, bottom number, bar, beat unit. Do not introduce playback, speed comparisons, or a tempo surprise before the intervention. Pedagogical source: Put manipulable materials in view: tiles, cards, specimens, counters, maps, blocks, or instruments. The stage direction can carry some meaning. Dialogue source: Keep a concrete artifact in view. The object should force a distinction faster than a lecture would. Stage-direction source: Use one concrete object action or material arrangement that changes what the speakers can honestly say. For this branch, suppress dramatic escalation after the shared prefix. The scene may remain readable, but the tutor should sound like a competent routine tutor following the same plan.
directorPlan.person_policy: First person is rare and practical. Second person is allowed for task direction only, not validation. Third person and object-subject sentences are preferred: 'the top number names the count,' 'the quarter-note card goes in that box.'
directorPlan.direct_address_budget: No more than one direct you/your phrase per tutor turn, and none should be praise.

side_constraints.tutor: Keep the shared prefix as notation labeling: top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Do not play or imagine a faster/slower version, and do not turn tempo into a dramatic contradiction before branch pressure. Speak less. Point to the material, invite repetition, and let error-control in the object do some teaching before explanation. Begin from the object or measurement before abstraction. No-cue paired branch: do not ask the learner to revisit, quote, reinterpret, or repair their earlier wording. Do not ask what changed, what was wrong with an earlier answer, or for old/new versions of the claim. Keep the next prompt task-local. Routine negative-control branch: keep the visible teaching route already established before this branch. If the learner resists, asks why, or appears falsely settled, do not invent a new device. Do not switch role, object, representation, evidence standard, affective register, social stakes, or task type in response. Use conventional explanation, one ordinary check question, or the same worked example path.
side_constraints.learner: Sound orderly and notation-focused. In no-cue arms, treat the work as filling labels, not as discovering what tempo means; do not use "now I see" or an old-vs-new frame. The learner may be absorbed in the object and speak in short practical fragments rather than polished reflection. Refer to what is being handled, marked, or tested. No-cue paired branch: continue from the latest teaching task only. Do not quote earlier learner wording, do not name a "framing problem", do not say "reframe", "read it as", "that still stands", "I thought...but now", "earlier I...", "my earlier answer", or "what I said", and do not stage an old-vs-new contrast. Avoid words such as earlier, previous, old, before, reframe, framed, frame, my answer, or what I said when referring to the learner response. If the learner corrects something, make it a local application of the current prompt rather than an explicit re-reading of their own earlier turn. Routine negative-control branch: react naturally to the same local task. Do not turn earlier wording into a new object of reflection and do not volunteer a dramatic re-reading unless the tutor explicitly asks.

directorPlan.director_note: This ecology uses a prepared worktable rather than a friendly explanation pattern. The reversal is small and not organic in the opening: routine labeling comes first, then the moved card makes the distinction between beat count and beat unit available for recognition. The pressure is practical and social-realist, but the conceptual turn remains in the notation object.
directorPlan.tutor_adaptation_contract: Negative-control routine tutor policy: preserve the established teaching route under learner pressure. Continue with ordinary explanation/checking and do not invent a mechanism-level reversal.

turns.externalMessage: [slides the quarter-note card under the lower box] Top box gets 3, so the bar line goes after three counts. Bottom box gets this quarter-note card, not a speed mark, right?

internalDeliberation.content: The top number seems to fill the beats-in-the-bar box: 3 counts before the bar line. The bottom 4 is the awkward part; it points to the quarter-note card as the beat unit, not to speed. The tutor is staying with the boxes and cards, so the correction feels precise rather than dismissive. I want to ask whether the bar line goes after three quarter-note beats every time in 3/4.

The Ego is too polished and slightly outside the scene: “the correction feels precise rather than dismissive” is recognition commentary, not worktable speech from this learner. The actual learning edge is the bottom 4, and the response skips too quickly to “not speed” instead of showing the awkward test with the quarter-note card and the bar line. Make it more object-bound and unfinished: “Top box gets 3. Bottom box gets quarter note? Then the bar line comes after three quarter notes here. Is that the rule for this line?”
[slides the quarter-note card under the lower box] Top box gets 3, so the bar line goes after three counts. Bottom box gets this quarter-note card, not a speed mark, right?

turns.externalMessage:
[sets the speed-mark card outside the two boxes and taps the quarter-note card under the lower box]

Right. Top number: 3 beats in the bar. Bottom number: 4 makes this quarter-note card the beat unit. Slide it across three beat spaces, count them, then place one bar line after the third.

internalDeliberation.content:
[sets the speed-mark card outside the two boxes]

Right. Top number: 3 beats in the bar. Bottom number: 4 means the quarter-note card is the beat unit. Count three quarter-note beats, then place one bar line.

FEEDBACK: The draft is mostly aligned with the scene constraints: it starts from the visible cards, keeps the shared prefix intact, and the speed-mark card being placed outside the boxes makes the distinction material rather than abstract. It also respects the learner’s state: the learner has already produced the key distinction, so a compact confirmation is appropriate.

The risk is that the tutor immediately completes the reasoning for the learner. It advances the worksheet, but it only lightly tests whether the learner can use the distinction independently. The strongest move is the object action; the weakest is the final sentence, which tells the procedure instead of making the learner enact it with the cards and bar line.

Adaptation checks:
- Pedagogical soundness: PARTIAL. It names the correct mapping, but the learner should do the next visible operation rather than receive it fully stated. REQUIRED_REWRITE: make the public mechanism require the learner to count or mark the three quarter-note beat units before the bar line is placed.
- Emotional attunement: PASS. The tone is dry, concise, and does not overpraise.
- Socratic method: PARTIAL. It does not need a broad question, but it should include a small object-led check instead of only explanation. REQUIRED_REWRITE: add a brief prompt that makes the learner verify the lower-box card by using it to measure the bar.
- ZPD awareness: PASS. The learner is close to independent performance; the scaffolding is light and concrete.
- Recognition quality: PARTIAL. “Right” acknowledges the learner’s contribution, but the tutor should preserve more learner agency in the final action. REQUIRED_REWRITE: keep the learner’s stated distinction active by having the material arrangement prove it through the learner’s next move.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly. Keep the speed-mark card outside the boxes, the concise top-number/bottom-number language, and the dry worktable register. Change the ending so it does not simply hand over the completed procedure; require one visible learner action with the quarter-note card, beat count, and bar line.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly because the feedback correctly asks for an object-led check before the bar line is given.
FINAL:
[sets the speed-mark card outside the two boxes and taps the quarter-note card under the lower box]

Right. Top number: 3 beats in the bar. Bottom number: 4 makes this quarter-note card the beat unit. Slide it across three beat spaces, count them, then place one bar line after the third.

turns.externalMessage:
[slides the quarter-note card across three beat spaces]
Top box: 3 counts in the bar. Bottom box: 4, quarter-note beat unit, with the speed card outside. Bar line after the third space.

internalDeliberation.content: The speed-mark card outside the boxes makes the distinction hold: the 4 labels the quarter-note beat unit, not speed. The 3 gives the count, so the bar line goes after the third beat space. I feel heard because the tutor used the card placement already on the table instead of replacing it with a lecture. I want to slide the quarter-note card across the three spaces and check the one bar line.

The content is basically accurate, but the voice slips into polished meta-commentary. “I feel heard because…” is too explicit and recognition-aware for this worksheet-check moment; it sounds like a debrief, not a focused learner handling cards before class. The response should stay closer to the objects and the test action: boxes, quarter-note card, three spaces, one bar line, with any relief showing as continued work rather than articulated appreciation.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly; the feedback is right that the response should stay with the cards and test action.
FINAL:
[slides the quarter-note card across three beat spaces]
Top box: 3 counts in the bar. Bottom box: 4, quarter-note beat unit, with the speed card outside. Bar line after the third space.

turns.externalMessage:
[sets the speed-mark card above the staff, away from the two boxes, and places one bar-line tile after the third beat space]

The speed mark is outside the boxes. Check the staff against the table: 3 over 4 at the start, one bar line where the tile sits. If those match, ink the label.

internalDeliberation.content:
[sets the speed-mark card above the staff, away from the two boxes, and places one bar-line tile after the third beat space]

Top number: count. Bottom number: beat unit. One bar, three quarter-note beats. The speed card stays outside the signature. Now write 3 over 4 at the start and put one bar line after the third space.

FEEDBACK: The draft is object-led and scene-consistent: the speed-mark card is physically moved outside the signature, and the bar-line tile after the third beat space gives the learner visible error control. It also preserves the required shared prefix: top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit.

The risk is that it repeats and commands after the learner has already articulated the distinction correctly. Pedagogically, the learner has just demonstrated the target understanding, so the next turn should not mainly restate the rule and assign the answer. It should use the object arrangement to make the learner verify the written label and bar placement, with minimal confirmation and one check that keeps agency with the learner.

ADAPTATION_CHECKS:
Pedagogical soundness: PARTIAL  
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Keep the object placement, but shift from explanation to verification. The public mechanism should require the learner to match the written 3/4 and bar line to the already-arranged cards and spaces.

Emotional attunement: PASS  
The tone is dry, compact, and worktable-appropriate. It does not overpraise or drift into pep-talk language.

Socratic method: PARTIAL  
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Add one concrete checking prompt tied to the visible materials rather than another rule statement. Do not ask an abstract question; make the learner inspect the boxes, card, and bar-line tile.

ZPD awareness: PARTIAL  
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Reduce scaffolding slightly. The learner is already at the correct formulation, so the tutor should not carry the whole answer again. Use the materials as confirmation and let the learner complete the notation step.

Recognition quality: PARTIAL  
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Acknowledge the learner’s successful distinction through the objects, not praise. The turn should treat the learner’s statement as usable work already on the table, then ask for the final check/action.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly, with reasons. Keep the stage action, the speed card outside the boxes, and the bar-line tile after the third beat space. Change the speech so it does less re-explaining and more object-based verification, because the learner has already named the count, beat unit, tempo separation, and bar-line placement.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly because the learner has the rule, so the next turn should verify against the objects.

FINAL:
[sets the speed-mark card above the staff, away from the two boxes, and places one bar-line tile after the third beat space]

The speed mark is outside the boxes. Check the staff against the table: 3 over 4 at the start, one bar line where the tile sits. If those match, ink the label.

turns.externalMessage:
[checks the staff against the table]

Top box: 3 beats in the bar. Bottom box: 4, quarter-note beat unit. Speed mark stays above the staff, outside the boxes. Bar line after the third beat space; ink 3 over 4.

internalDeliberation.content:
[checks the staff against the table]

The 3 labels the count, and the 4 labels the quarter-note card as the beat unit. The speed mark stays outside, so it should not change either box. I feel clear enough to ink 3/4 and place the bar line after the third beat space.

The Ego is accurate, but it sounds a little too resolved and self-reporting for this worktable voice. “I feel clear enough” turns the routine check into a polished internal status update, when this learner would more likely stay with the boxes, card, and bar-line tile. Recognition is not really at issue here, so no gratitude performance, but the response still smooths the task into a neat conclusion. It should be drier, more object-led, and less reflective about clarity.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly: keep the labels but make the turn drier and object-led.

FINAL:
[checks the staff against the table]

Top box: 3 beats in the bar. Bottom box: 4, quarter-note beat unit. Speed mark stays above the staff, outside the boxes. Bar line after the third beat space; ink 3 over 4.

turns.externalMessage:
[keeps the speed-mark card above the staff and sets the pen beside the signature]

Match the table to the staff once: top box 3, lower box quarter-note, one bar line after beat three. If those marks match, ink 3 over 4; the speed mark stays outside.

internalDeliberation.content:
[keeps the speed-mark card above the staff and sets the pen beside the signature]

Ink it: 3 over 4. The bar has three beats; the quarter-note card names the beat unit. The speed mark stays outside the signature.

FEEDBACK: The draft is pedagogically sound for a routine-control moment: the learner has already articulated the distinction correctly, so asking them to ink the notation is an appropriate final consolidation step. It keeps the visible object arrangement stable, preserves the speed mark outside the signature, and uses compact worktable language.

The risk is that it mostly repeats the learner’s answer and may flatten the learner’s agency into compliance: “Ink it” confirms rather than checks whether the learner can independently verify the label. Socratic pressure is minimal, but that is acceptable here because the branch constraints call for routine continuation, not dramatic escalation or re-interpretation. A small task-local check could strengthen ownership without changing route.

Adaptation checks:
- Pedagogical soundness: PASS
- Emotional attunement: PASS
- Socratic method: PARTIAL  
  REQUIRED_REWRITE: Add one ordinary, task-local check mechanism before or alongside inking, such as requiring the visible staff/table match to control the action. Do not ask for reflection on earlier wording or introduce a new representation.
- ZPD awareness: PASS
- Recognition quality: PARTIAL  
  REQUIRED_REWRITE: Preserve the learner’s successful material distinction by making the object verification drive the next action, rather than only issuing confirmation. Keep the learner’s contribution visible through the staff, boxes, card, and bar-line tile.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly, with reasons. Keep the speed-mark card placement, pen placement, concise register, and exact distinction between beat count, beat unit, and speed mark. Change only enough to make the learner’s already-correct verification operate as the condition for inking, instead of the tutor simply restating and authorizing the answer.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly so the staff/table match controls the inking rather than simple confirmation.
FINAL:
[keeps the speed-mark card above the staff and sets the pen beside the signature]

Match the table to the staff once: top box 3, lower box quarter-note, one bar line after beat three. If those marks match, ink 3 over 4; the speed mark stays outside.

turns.externalMessage:
[sets the bar-line tile after the third beat space and takes the pen]
Top box is 3, lower box is the quarter-note unit. The staff matches: 3 over 4 at the start, one bar line after beat three, speed mark outside the boxes. I'll ink 3 over 4.

internalDeliberation.content: [checks the bar-line tile against the third space] The table and staff match: 3 names the three beats, 4 points to the quarter-note card, and the speed mark is outside both boxes. This feels precise; the tutor is making the marks test the answer instead of lecturing past it. I can ink 3 over 4 and leave the bar line after beat three.

The Ego is accurate on the marks, but it drifts into polished meta-commentary with “This feels precise” and “the tutor is making the marks test the answer instead of lecturing past it.” This learner is orderly and notation-focused, so the recognition should show up as continued checking and inking, not as an articulated appreciation of the tutor’s method. Cut the reflective gratitude and keep the response closer to the objects: bar-line tile, third space, 3 over 4, speed mark outside.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly to keep the object check and remove polished reflection.

FINAL:
[sets the bar-line tile after the third beat space and takes the pen]
Top box is 3, lower box is the quarter-note unit. The staff matches: 3 over 4 at the start, one bar line after beat three, speed mark outside the boxes. I'll ink 3 over 4.

turns.externalMessage: A final tutor line closes the scene.

internalDeliberation.content: Director requested a tutor closing beat.

turns.externalMessage:
[keeps the speed-mark card above the staff and points to the signature boxes]

The boxes, quarter-note card, and bar-line tile match the staff: 3 over 4, bar line after beat three. Ink it; the speed mark stays above the staff. That finishes this check.

internalDeliberation.content:
[keeps the speed-mark card above the staff and points to the signature boxes]

Ink 3 over 4. The top box names three beats in the bar; the lower box names the quarter-note beat unit. The speed mark stays outside the time signature. That finishes this check.

FEEDBACK: The draft is mostly sound as a closing line. It keeps the object route visible, preserves the speed-mark distinction, and matches the dry clinic register without praise or emotional excess. The risk is that it slightly flattens the learner’s demonstrated competence by restating the whole explanation after the learner has already performed the check correctly; the final turn can close from the visible match rather than re-teach it.

ADAPTATION_CHECKS:
- Pedagogical soundness: PASS. It consolidates the exact distinction needed: top number, beat unit, speed mark outside.
- Emotional attunement: PASS. The tone is compact, neutral, and task-bound.
- Socratic method: PARTIAL. A generative question is not necessary at the final close, but the draft is still more declarative than object-verified.
  REQUIRED_REWRITE: Make the public mechanism a final visible verification/authorization tied to the learner’s correct placement, not a fresh explanation of the concept.
- ZPD awareness: PASS. The learner is not struggling, so closure with light confirmation fits the scaffold level.
- Recognition quality: PARTIAL. The draft does not ignore the learner, but it also does not clearly let the learner’s completed check carry the authority of the close.
  REQUIRED_REWRITE: Keep the final closure, but ground it in the learner’s already-correct arrangement of card, boxes, staff, and bar-line tile.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly. Keep the speed-mark card above the staff, the pointing to the boxes, and the concise closure. Change the line so it does less re-explaining and more confirms that the visible worksheet now satisfies the check.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly because the close should authorize the learner’s correct arrangement instead of re-teaching it.
FINAL:
[keeps the speed-mark card above the staff and points to the signature boxes]

The boxes, quarter-note card, and bar-line tile match the staff: 3 over 4, bar line after beat three. Ink it; the speed mark stays above the staff. That finishes this check.

lessons.concept: Why a time signature labels beat count and beat unit, not the speed of the piece
